title: Going Places by A. R. Barton
subject: English

notes.heading: Theme
notes.text: Fantasy versus reality, adolescent dreams, class barriers, and escapism.

summary:
“Going Places” is about Sophie, a teenage girl from a poor working-class family who escapes her dull reality through fantasies. She lives with her parents, her practical brother Geoff, and little Derek. Unlike her friend Jansie, who accepts that they are destined for the biscuit factory, Sophie dreams big. She wants to own a boutique, be an actress, or become a fashion designer.

Sophie idolizes her silent, secretive brother Geoff, who works as a mechanic and travels to places she can only imagine. To impress him, she invents a story that she met Danny Casey, a young Irish footballer she hero-worships, in the arcade. She says he asked for her autograph and promised to meet her again. Geoff believes her, but Jansie spreads the story. Sophie’s father dismisses it as another wild story and warns her not to dream beyond her class.

But Sophie convinces herself that the story is true. She dresses up and waits for Danny by the canal on Saturday, imagining every detail: his green eyes, soft voice, and the way he would come through the crowd. She waits till evening, but he never shows up. Though heartbroken, she still cannot accept reality. She walks home imagining what she will tell Geoff and telling herself Danny will come next week.

The title is ironic. Sophie dreams of “going places” in life, but she only goes to wait for a fantasy. The story shows the pain of adolescence, the gap between dreams and reality, and how hero-worship helps poor teenagers cope with limited futures.
